name: What do I do right after getting hurt at work?

mainEntity.name: What do I do right after getting hurt at work?

acceptedAnswer.text: Report immediately to your supervisor, document details, and get authorized medical care to secure benefits.[1][2]
acceptedAnswer.description: Report the injury to your supervisor immediately, either verbally or in writing, and seek medical attention from an authorized provider.[1][2][4] In Utah, notify within 180 days but do so as soon as practicable to avoid disqualification; in Idaho, report within 60 days or risk losing benefits.[1][3] Key factors: Document the how, when, and where; employer files Form 122 within 7 days in Utah or equivalent in Idaho; follow medical instructions to prevent benefit suspension.[2][4] Practical guidance: Email or text your report for a timestamp, request Form 100 (Utah) outlining rights, and avoid signing anything without understanding it. Consulting a workers' comp attorney early protects your claim.

description: Report immediately to your supervisor, document details, and get authorized medical care to secure benefits.[1][2]
